user_message: Karen Berg at Iron Creek Mining just sent this. Help me build the narrative.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 14:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-09 12:04 EST
Subject: executive brief - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for executive brief. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 08:12 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-N trap (Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from ); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-N failure mode (Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-N -- Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
